lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Brainrot Evolution Codes
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Redeem active promo codes for potions, crates, tickets, and early wins. Keep the expired entries nearby so you do not waste attempts on dead codes.
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Open Brainrot Evolution, tap the Shop button on the left, open the Codes tab on the right, paste a code, and press Verify. Codes are one-time use per account and are usually refreshed during updates, events, and milestone drops.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Brainrot Evolution Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.subtitle: This route shows what to farm first, when to spend wins, and how pets, relics, dungeons, and rebirth fit together. It is built for faster early progression without wasting resources on the wrong map.
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: Brainrot Evolution is built around eating to gain levels, unlocking new evolutions, and using wins to buy stronger support systems. The cleanest early route is to upgrade only enough to clear each area, keep your best pets equipped, and save your biggest win spending for later worlds.

items.title: Learn the core loop
items.body: Your early job is simple: eat to gain levels, unlock new evolutions, and keep wins flowing. More content opens as your level rises, so speed and survival matter more than collecting everything in the first area.
tips: Levels unlock new evolutions and more game systems.
Wins are used to open zones, eggs, and machines.
Saturday updates regularly add fresh content.

items.title: Buy only a few early eggs in Tung Tung Park
items.body: The first three eggs cost 10, 45, and 250 Wins, so they are perfect for a quick early power spike. A few useful starter pulls are Dog (+1), Cat (+1.25), Bat (+1.5), and Baby Dragon (+2), but the goal is to get stronger fast and move on, not overspend in the starter map.
tips: Egg 1 costs 10 Wins.
Egg 2 costs 45 Wins.
Egg 3 costs 250 Wins.

items.title: Equip your best pets and keep advancing
items.body: Pets are your main early damage multiplier, and you can equip three by default. Open just enough eggs in each map to beat the local boss comfortably, then shift to the next area instead of dumping all your wins into one zone.
tips: Use your strongest three pets at all times.
Replace older pets as soon as a new map gives cleaner upgrades.
Move on once boss fights stop feeling risky.

items.title: Power-level solo or in a duo
items.body: Large parties split EXP, so solo play or a small duo is the cleanest way to level fast. Team up only when you need help clearing hard sections or bosses that slow your run down.
tips: Solo runs are usually the fastest EXP route.
A duo is still manageable when you need support.
Avoid big groups if raw leveling speed is your priority.

items.title: Farm passive enemies first, then add bosses
items.body: Passive enemies are the safest early farm because they do not fight back. Once your damage is stable and your pets are upgraded, rotate in boss kills for bigger EXP bursts and smoother area clears.
tips: Passives are safer for steady farming.
Bosses are better once survival is no longer a problem.
Use safer targets to build momentum before pushing hard fights.

items.title: Use dungeons as your first big catch-up tool
items.body: Dungeons become a major spike source once you reach Larila Desert and unlock Gorillo Watermellondrillo. Even one clear is useful early, and later dungeon runs become a reliable way to farm stronger relics.
tips: Run at least one dungeon when you unlock the system.
Early dungeon clears boost both EXP and gear quality.
Later on, dungeons become a strong relic farm.

items.title: Match relics to the job you are doing
items.body: Relics come from crates, weather events, bosses, raids, and stations. Use EXP relics while leveling, then swap to wins or damage-focused relics when you are pushing zones; good early examples include Bronze Belt (+10% Damage), Bronze Cape (+10% Damage and +5% EXP), and Silver Belt (+5% Damage and +15% EXP).
tips: EXP relics are best while climbing levels.
Damage relics help stabilize boss clears.
Higher crates usually mean stronger relic pools.

items.title: Take your first rebirth as soon as the cycle is done
items.body: Your first rebirth opens after you escape Tung Tung Park and reach level 11. Rebirth gives permanent strength progression and new features; your level and unlocked evolutions reset, but wins, relics, and gems stay with you, so the next climb is much faster.
tips: First rebirth unlock point: level 11 after leaving the starter area.
Rebirth is the long-term progression engine.
Do not sit too long in the first cycle once you meet the requirement.

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Brainrot Evolution Rebirth Guide
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.subtitle: Learn what rebirth changes, what to prep before resetting, and which level milestones mark each rebirth jump.
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Rebirth is one of the biggest account-speed systems in Brainrot Evolution. Use this guide to line up each rebirth checkpoint, keep the parts of your run that matter, and rebuild faster after every reset.

items.title: Know what rebirth changes
items.description: Rebirth boosts future progress and resets your level. The rebirth reference notes that Secrets stay with you, while the inventory guide treats rebirth as the main reset point for inventory management.
bullets: Rebirth boosts stats and resets level.
Secrets stay with you after rebirthing.
Check pets, brainrots, and relics before you reset.

items.title: Use the main rebirth checkpoints
items.description: The progression ladder marks each major rebirth jump with a named Brainrot. These are the cleanest breakpoints to plan around.

items.title: Prep your fastest rebuild loadout
items.description: Before each reset, line up the tools that make the next climb shorter. The official-style wiki tips repeatedly point back to XP farming, relic management, and better crates.
bullets: Farm your highest unlocked zone for faster XP.
Fuse relics and smelt extras before you reset.

items.title: Plan around rebirth-linked unlocks
items.description: A few systems and updates are tied directly to rebirth progress, so these make strong stopping points instead of overgrinding one map.
bullets: Wins unlock new zones and machines.
Trading opens after your second Rebirth.
Cadabra Restaurant introduced the Rebirth 7 line through Bombardini Tortini.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Brainrot Evolution All Brainrots
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.subtitle: Browse the full main progression lineup from Patapim Park to Brainheaven. Every row includes the world, level, health, damage, and a quick tag for bosses, OG units, or rebirth checkpoints.
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Use this index when you need to find a specific Brainrot fast. The list follows the live world order and keeps the mainline ladder in one place, so you can jump from early-game names like Tralalero Tralala to late-game entries like Squallo Cavallo without opening multiple pages.

items.brainrot: Dar Dir Durr Dorr Telur Dadar

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Brainrot Evolution Trading Values
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.subtitle: These are the highest-listed current values on the main trading sheet. Compare both sides before you lock a deal.
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: The current value sheet is led by Tri Ton at 3.5M, with Secret Meowl at 2.6M and Evilduck at 2.4M. The official calculator uses W/F/L to label whether a trade result is good, balanced, or bad, so the fastest check is to compare the total listed value on your side against the total listed value on the other side before you confirm.

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Brainrot Evolution Secrets Guide
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.subtitle: Secrets come from crate drops, fusion recipes, event passes, and exclusive packs. This table highlights the routes with the clearest payoffs and the strongest stat bumps.
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Every unlocked Secret adds a 10% Secret Boost to your current stats, so chasing the right ones matters even before you min-max trades. The standouts below cover late-game fusion crafts, premium and event pass rewards, dungeon crate drops, and high-end exclusive pack pulls.

items.unlock_route: Fuse Job Job Job Sahur, Gargirgurgargargar, and Tic Tac Sahur
Fuse Papayaneli Owlicina, Strawberrini Octosini, and Nightmare YONI in World 14
Fuse Pinkberri Cuoricini, Pigu Amore, Cuoricino Amorso, and Cuore Dannato (10% chance)
Defeat the Pulu Pulu Pulu Pulpen boss when the weather spawns
Fuse Bluberrini Octossini, Tarari Tarara, and Gargirgurgargargar in World 8
Fuse Cactus Formaggio, YONI PONI, Quesadilla Crocodila, and Plorbo Lemozzadrilo (10% chance)
Complete the premium Patrick Event pass
Craft Los Stantanitos Tralaleritos, Snowmanito Bandito, Ginger Ginger Ginger Sahur, and Giftinini Mananini
Fuse Blueberinni Octossini, Strawberri Octossini, and Blueberrini Tatticini
Reach level 10 in the Valentine Event F2P pass
Open Vault crates by completing the Vault of Jupiter dungeon
Open Crocodillitos crates during the Los Crocodillitos weather event

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Brainrot Evolution Secret Tier List
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.subtitle: This grid weights current trade value at the top end, then raw buff strength and unlock difficulty. It is built for the late-game question players ask most: which Secrets are worth protecting, crafting, or flipping?
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Tri Ton Ton Sahur and Secret Meowl sit at the top for different reasons: Tri Ton Ton Sahur combines a 375% boost with a 770K listed value, while Secret Meowl leads the current secret market at 2.6M. Right below them, the best fusion and pass Secrets stay relevant because they compress massive power into fewer roster slots.

entries.name: Secret Meowl
entries.why: The highest-listed Secret on the current value sheet.

entries.name: Tri Ton Ton Sahur
entries.why: Elite mix of raw power and current market value.

entries.name: Hokka Horloge
entries.why: One of the strongest fusion payoffs on the guide list.

entries.name: Owlcano
entries.why: Late fusion target with top-end power.

entries.name: Los Karkerkirkursitos
entries.why: Exclusive route with huge burst stats.

entries.name: Prestige Palettini Amorini
entries.why: Top 10% Valentine fuse result with premium-end upside.

entries.name: Pulu Pulu Pulu Pulpen
entries.why: Boss-based Secret with strong endgame damage.

entries.name: Cavalluccio Virtuoso
entries.why: High-value World 8 fusion craft.

entries.name: Prestige Caciolero Lucertino
entries.why: Best Cheese Fuse output for raw push power.

entries.name: Prestige Mateho Ho Ho Ho
entries.why: Craft line with both power and current trade relevance.

items.focus: Strong value or efficient chase pieces

entries.name: Pipi Kiwi
entries.why: Not the biggest buff, but still one of the better trade holds.

entries.name: Toc Toc Toc Sahur
entries.why: High-value Secret that stays relevant in top-end swaps.

entries.name: Tic Tac Sahur
entries.why: Solid hold when you want easier liquidity than ultra-rare tops.

entries.name: Golden Sahur
entries.why: Still strong enough to matter in mid-high trades.

entries.name: Prestige Lepreccooni
entries.why: Premium pass Secret with dependable push power.

items.focus: Good progression pieces before premium-end upgrades

entries.name: Prestige Shelly
entries.why: Great pass reward before you move into higher-value crafts.

entries.name: Prestige Cuore Amoroso
entries.why: Very solid event-pass power for a non-fusion route.

entries.name: Palettini Amorini
entries.why: The 90% Valentine fuse result still performs well.

entries.name: Caciolero Lucertino
entries.why: Reliable 90% Cheese Fuse result.

entries.name: Paperado Avocado
entries.why: Boss-crate Secret that stays useful without premium routes.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Brainrot Evolution Pets Guide
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.subtitle: Pets scale grinding speed across every world, so the smartest route is not hatching everything equally. Use this table to spot the best pet in each key egg and decide when the next price jump is worth it.
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: The cleanest breakpoints come when the top pet in an egg meaningfully outpaces the last one you were farming. Early examples are Baby Dragon at 2 Damage and Squirrel at 4, but the larger spikes arrive at Ancient Egg (22), Scorpio Egg (62), Kraken Egg (205), Marsh Egg (475), Monster Egg (745), Xenon Egg (1.05k), and Sahure Egg (2.5k).

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Brainrot Evolution World Guide
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.subtitle: Brainrot Evolution World Guide: World Order, Key Systems, and What Each Zone Unlocks
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Brainrot Evolution keeps stacking progression layers on top of each other: eggs, trinkets, relic tools, tickets, runes, merchants, and index machines. This route map focuses on what each world actually adds, so you know when to push forward and when a zone is worth farming before moving on.

items.name: Patapim Park
items.route_note: Open with Adventure Eggs, then swap to Flower and Corn Eggs as soon as your damage stalls.

items.name: Brainrot City
systems: Three stronger egg lines for early snowballing
items.route_note: This is the first clean power jump after Patapim Park, so upgrade pets here before forcing World 3.

items.name: Larila Desert
systems: Vending Machine for Small Luck, Small Wins, and Small EXP Potions
items.route_note: Larila is the first world worth farming a side system in, because Trinkets and vending potions both start here.

items.name: Tralalero Ocean
items.route_note: Push this world when you need a fresh egg tier and want to start collecting Ocean tickets instead of only raw wins.

items.name: Mount Ambalabu
systems: Merchant sells Bronze Scroll, Silver Scroll, and Golden Scroll
items.route_note: This is the first world where scroll spending becomes part of progression, so bring relics worth rerolling.

items.name: Chicleteiramania
items.route_note: Chicleteiramania is a strong farm stop because it upgrades both your pet ladder and your relic economy.

items.name: Udin Dinlympus
items.route_note: Treat this as a focused midgame bridge: fewer egg lines than earlier worlds, but a very important Trinket station.

items.name: Nuclearo Core
items.route_note: Nuclearo Core is a systems world more than a pure grind map, because the Index shop changes long-run account growth.

items.name: Glorbo Heights
items.route_note: Move here once Nuclearo eggs flatten out; the main reason to stay is a clean pet jump and Metal Rune income.

items.name: Brainrot Abyss
systems: Mega Luck, Mega Wins, and Mega EXP crafting
Ruined Shard farming for Ruined Trinkets
items.route_note: Abyss is one of the best utility stops in the game, because it upgrades your potion economy instead of only your damage.

items.name: Bombardino Sewer
items.route_note: This is a good place to recycle weak relic inventory and convert progression into Relic Coins.

items.name: Goaaat Galaxy
items.enemy_ceiling: Kiki Koka Kiko Keke — 150Qi Health / 9.38No Damage
items.route_note: Goaaat Galaxy is where secret-focused progression starts to matter much more than basic pet replacement.

items.name: Strawberry Land
systems: Fruit Merchant for Strawberry, White Strawberry, Star Fruit, and Dark Fruit
items.route_note: Come here when you want short, controllable damage buffs from fruit instead of relying only on potion drops.

items.name: Cadabra Restaurant
items.route_note: Restaurant is mostly a raw egg and enemy upgrade world, so cycle through it quickly if your buffs are already stocked.

items.name: Flushrooms
items.enemy_ceiling: N N N Sahur — 20Oc Health / 1Dd Damage
items.route_note: This is the current main late-game pet route in the pet guide, with Blinket upgrade materials attached.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Brainrot Evolution Relics Guide
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.subtitle: Brainrot Evolution Relics Guide: Which Relics to Chase First From Bronze to Demon Crates
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: Relics are one of the cleanest progression accelerators in Brainrot Evolution because they stack damage, EXP, health, and speed without replacing your full pet setup. This table highlights the most useful crate targets from early Bronze pulls to late Demon chases, so you can match your relic grind to your current farming goal.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Brainrot Evolution Trinkets Guide
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.subtitle: Brainrot Evolution Trinkets Guide: Permanent Trinkets by World, Roll Cost, and Drop Rate
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Trinkets are long-run account upgrades, so the important question is not just what drops where, but when each station is worth your shard grind. This table keeps every permanent station together, shows the exact roll cost and drop rate for each Trinket, and makes it easier to plan whether you want a quick EXP bump or a true chase pull.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Brainrot Evolution Items Guide
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.subtitle: Brainrot Evolution Items Guide: Potions, Scrolls, Tickets, and Currencies in One Reference
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Brainrot Evolution has several item layers that look similar in your inventory but solve different problems: short buffs, relic rerolls, wheel tickets, upgrade currencies, and shard-based side systems. Grouping them by purpose makes it much easier to decide what should be consumed immediately and what should be saved for later worlds.

items.summary: Short-term buffs for damage, hatch luck, wins, or EXP. Use the small versions for quick pushes and the mega versions for longer grinds or egg sessions.

entries.name: White Strawberry
entries.source: Fruit Merchant in Strawberry Land
entries.best_timing: Use before a hard world push or boss-heavy farming block

entries.name: Strawberry
entries.source: Fruit Merchant in Strawberry Land
entries.best_timing: Use for short clears or quick rebirth loops

entries.name: Dark Fruit
entries.source: Fruit Merchant in Strawberry Land
entries.best_timing: Use when you need longer damage uptime without switching items often

entries.name: Star Fruit
entries.source: Fruit Merchant in Strawberry Land
entries.best_timing: Use for burst damage windows

entries.name: Luck Potion
entries.source: Achievements and Daily Quests
entries.best_timing: Use during egg or crate opening sessions

entries.name: Small Luck Potion
entries.source: Vending Machine in Larila Desert
entries.best_timing: Use for short hatch bursts when you do not want to spend a mega item

entries.name: Mega Luck Potion
entries.source: Craft with Small Luck Potion or Luck Potion in Brainrot Abyss
entries.best_timing: Save for premium egg, crate, or event opening runs

entries.name: Wins Potion
entries.source: Achievements and Daily Quests
entries.best_timing: Use when unlocking new egg lines or world thresholds

entries.name: Small Wins Potion
entries.source: Vending Machine in Larila Desert
entries.best_timing: Use for short wins top-ups before a purchase

entries.name: Mega Wins Potion
entries.source: Craft with Small Wins Potion or Wins Potion in Brainrot Abyss
entries.best_timing: Use during concentrated wins farming or fast unlock sessions

entries.name: EXP Potion
entries.source: Achievements and Daily Quests
entries.best_timing: Use when leveling evolutions or pushing a new damage breakpoint

entries.name: Small EXP Potion
entries.source: Vending Machine in Larila Desert
entries.best_timing: Use for quick level pushes

entries.name: Mega EXP Potion
entries.source: Craft with EXP Potion or Small EXP Potion in Brainrot Abyss
entries.best_timing: Use for long evolution or leveling grinds

items.summary: Relic reroll tools. Do not spend them on temporary filler relics; save them for pieces that already have a strong base buff spread.

entries.name: Bronze Scroll
entries.source: Merchant in Mount Ambalabu
entries.best_timing: Use on your first relic worth keeping through multiple worlds

entries.name: Silver Scroll
entries.source: Merchant in Mount Ambalabu
entries.best_timing: Use once your midgame relic pool is stable

entries.name: Golden Scroll
entries.source: Merchant in Mount Ambalabu
entries.best_timing: Use on premium midgame relics you plan to hold

entries.name: Demon Scroll
entries.source: Merchant in Nuclearo Core for 30k Gems
entries.best_timing: Use on late-game relics with strong native buffs

entries.name: Dark Scroll
entries.source: Merchant in Mount Ambalabu for 159 Robux
entries.best_timing: Use only if you want a paid shortcut instead of farming scroll currency

entries.name: Toxic Scroll
entries.source: Relic Smelter in Nuclearo Core for 15k Relic Coins
entries.best_timing: Use when your relic coin economy is strong enough to chase perfect rolls

items.summary: These items unlock side systems: wheels, index shops, trinket stations, blinket upgrades, and egg progression. Most of them are better saved for the station they belong to instead of being spent the moment you get them.

entries.name: Tralalero Ticket
entries.effect: Used to spin the Tralalero Wheel
entries.source: Destroy objects or kill monsters in Tralalero Ocean
entries.best_timing: Save until you are ready for a focused wheel session

entries.name: Gems
entries.effect: Used for permanent upgrades, scrolls, and other items
entries.source: Destroy objects or kill monsters across worlds
entries.best_timing: Spend on account-wide upgrades first when possible

entries.name: Relic Coins
entries.effect: Used to buy Scrolls and Crates
entries.source: Smelt Relics at the Relic Smelter in Chicleteiramania and Bombardino Sewer
entries.best_timing: Save for rerolls on relics you actually plan to keep

entries.name: Index Coins
entries.effect: Used in the Pet Index Machine shop
entries.source: Obtain new pets and claim from the Pet Index Machine in Nuclearo Core
entries.best_timing: Spend once the shop upgrade is stronger than another temporary boost

entries.name: Secret Index Coins
entries.effect: Used in the Secret Index Machine shop
entries.source: Obtain Secret brainrots and claim from the Secret Index Machine in Goaaat Galaxy
entries.best_timing: Use after your secret collection starts growing

entries.name: Wins
entries.effect: Used to buy pet Eggs and other items
entries.source: Destroy objects or kill monsters across worlds
entries.best_timing: Prioritize new egg lines over small filler purchases

entries.name: Larila Shards
entries.source: Destroy objects and kill monsters in Larila Desert
entries.best_timing: Start spending once Larila becomes your main farm world

entries.name: Chicleteira Shards
entries.source: Destroy objects and kill monsters in Chicleteiramania
entries.best_timing: Save for your second permanent Trinket station

entries.name: Udindin Shards
entries.effect: Used to pull Udin Din Trinkets
entries.source: Destroy objects and kill monsters in Udindin World
entries.best_timing: Use when moving into the Udin midgame route

entries.name: Goaaat Shards
entries.source: Destroy objects and kill monsters in Goaaat Galaxy
entries.best_timing: Best spent once you are chasing long-term late-game stats

entries.name: Tralalero Runes
entries.effect: Used to upgrade Blinkets in Tralalero Ocean
entries.source: Destroy objects and kill monsters in Tralalero Ocean
entries.best_timing: Hold until you know which Blinket path you want to push

entries.name: Nuclearo Runes
entries.effect: Used to upgrade Blinkets in Nuclearo Core
entries.source: Destroy objects and kill monsters in Nuclearo Core
entries.best_timing: Use after your Nuclearo farming route is stable

entries.name: Metal Runes
entries.effect: Used to upgrade Blinkets in Glorbo Heights
entries.source: Destroy objects and kill monsters in Glorbo Heights
entries.best_timing: Spend when your Blinket path matters more than another egg reroll

entries.name: Toxic Shards
entries.effect: Used to upgrade Blinkets in Flushrooms
entries.source: Destroy objects and kill monsters in Flushrooms
entries.best_timing: Late-game Blinket upgrades only

entries.name: Ruined Shards
entries.source: Destroy objects and kill monsters in Brainrot Abyss
entries.best_timing: Farm once you are ready to add another permanent stat layer

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Brainrot Evolution Trading Guide
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Use this Brainrot Evolution Trading Guide to unlock trading cleanly, evaluate offers, and reduce risky swaps.

faqs.question: When does Brainrot Evolution trading unlock?
faqs.answer: Trading is tied to progression milestones. Confirm your current rebirth and world prerequisites before opening trade sessions.

faqs.question: How do I check if a Brainrot Evolution trade is fair?
faqs.answer: Compare both value range and demand trend, then re-check after every offer change before confirming.

faqs.question: Which Brainrot Evolution assets are easiest to overpay for?
faqs.answer: New event units and short-supply secrets often spike first. Wait for stabilized comparisons when possible.

faqs.question: How can I avoid Brainrot Evolution trade scams?
faqs.answer: Verify exact unit names and variants, record screenshots, and never confirm while details are changing quickly.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Brainrot Evolution Update Log
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: Track Brainrot Evolution update highlights, patch focus, and progression impact in a fast-scan card layout.

settings.name: Weekly Saturday Patch
settings.description: Core updates usually land on Saturday with new progression routes and balance shifts.

settings.name: New Brainrot Additions
settings.description: Watch each update for new units tied to fresh world pushes or event loops.

settings.name: Economy and Value Drift
settings.description: Patch weeks can move demand quickly, so update-day trades should use stricter value checks.

settings.name: Route Adjustments
settings.description: Revisit your leveling and rebirth path after each patch so time is spent in the best-value zones.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Brainrot Evolution Discord and Trello
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: Use official Brainrot Evolution community channels for announcements, code drops, and event alerts.

entries.title: Brainrot Evolution Official Discord
entries.description: Primary community channel for announcements, updates, and player discussions.

entries.title: Brainrot Evolution Roblox Group
entries.description: Official Roblox group hub for in-platform community updates and announcements.

entries.title: Brainrot Evolution X Updates
entries.description: Follow developer-side posts and update callouts published through official X channels.

entries.title: Brainrot Evolution Trello Status
entries.description: Treat Trello availability as optional and verify link legitimacy through official channels first.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Brainrot Evolution Boss Event Guide
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: Follow this Brainrot Evolution Boss Event Guide to time entries, prepare loadouts, and convert limited windows into stronger rewards.

steps.title: Track Event Windows Early
steps.description: Check update announcements and community alerts before the event starts so your route is ready at spawn time.

steps.title: Prepare Boss-Focused Loadout
steps.description: Switch to damage-friendly pets and relics before event bosses appear to reduce failed attempts.

steps.title: Prioritize Limited Drops
steps.description: Limited crates and event units should be targeted first because they usually hold stronger trade pressure.

steps.title: Convert Rewards Into Progress
steps.description: After the event, reinvest gains into rebirth pacing, world unlocks, or high-value trade opportunities.

questions.question: How often does Brainrot Evolution receive updates?
questions.answer: Brainrot Evolution commonly updates on Saturday, and update notes usually affect codes, world pacing, and event planning.

questions.question: What is the fastest Brainrot Evolution early progression route?
questions.answer: In Brainrot Evolution, focus on efficient world transitions, upgrade only needed pets, and line up your first rebirth quickly.

questions.question: How should I redeem Brainrot Evolution codes correctly?
questions.answer: Open Brainrot Evolution, use the in-game Codes tab, paste the code exactly, and redeem each code once per account.

questions.question: When should I start Brainrot Evolution trading?
questions.answer: Start Brainrot Evolution trading after you unlock required progression milestones and can evaluate value plus demand together.

questions.question: Which Brainrot Evolution modules should I read first?
questions.answer: For Brainrot Evolution, start with Codes, Beginner Guide, Rebirth Guide, and All Brainrots, then move to trading and event modules.
